name: Transandalus: Uleila del Campo Loop
name_original: Vuelta por Uleila del Campo — Transandalus
description: Explore the Transandalus: Uleila del Campo Loop, a moderate 14.6-mile mountain bike route with varied terrain and scenic Andalusian views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Uleila Del Campo features routes through mountainous terrain and arid landscapes typical of the region. The area is characterized by significant elevation changes, including mountain passes and high viewpoints. Trails often traverse open country, offering expansive views of the surrounding environment. The network of routes provides options for various skill levels, from moderate paths to challenging climbs. Best mountain bike trails around Uleila Del Campo The most popular mountain bike trail is Transandalus: Uleila del Campo Loop, a 14.6 miles (23.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete, offering a moderate circular route. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Uleila del Campo – Alto de Velefique Pass loop from Uleila del Campo, a difficult 50.6 miles (81.4 km) path that includes substantial climbs to a mountain pass. Local mountain bikers also love the Las Cruces Viewpoint – Viewpoint of the Hanging Houses loop from Sorbas, a 31.9 miles (51.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 4 hours 33 minutes. Mountain biking around Uleila Del Campo is defined by mountain passes, high viewpoints, and arid landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Uleila Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Uleila Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Uleila Del Campo?
A: There are 10 mountain bike trails documented on komoot for the Uleila Del Campo area, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Uleila Del Campo?
A: While there are no trails explicitly rated as 'easy', there are 7 moderate routes that could be suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032210921/transandalus-uleila-del-campo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus: Uleila del Campo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.6-mile circular route that takes less than two hours to complete.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Uleila Del Campo?
A: The trails around Uleila Del Campo are characterized by mountainous terrain and arid landscapes, featuring significant elevation changes, mountain passes, and high viewpoints. You'll often traverse open country with expansive views.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, there are 3 difficult routes for experienced mountain bikers. A notable challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uleila del Campo – Alto de Velefique Pass loop from Uleila del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a demanding 50.6-mile path with substantial climbs.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that form a loop?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails in the area are loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31552508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uleila del Campo loop from Uleila del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 24.9-mile circular route, perfect for exploring the local scenery.

Q: What are some interesting places to see or landmarks near the mountain bike trails?
A: While riding, you might encounter several interesting points. Nearby attractions include the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3589750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sorbas&lt;/a&gt;, the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lubrín Village and Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4995439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Cabeza de Monteagud Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tahal Castle&lt;/a&gt; also offers a glimpse into the region's history.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Uleila Del Campo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Uleila Del Campo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the challenging climbs, and the expansive views of the arid landscape.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails that offer varied terrain?
A: Yes, a good example of a moderate route with varied terrain is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38289005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sorbas – Uleila del Campo loop from Sorbas&lt;/a&gt;. This 23.3-mile trail offers a diverse riding experience through the region's characteristic landscapes.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike rides in this area?
A: Ride durations vary significantly based on the trail's length and difficulty. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032210921/transandalus-uleila-del-campo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus: Uleila del Campo Loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under 2 hours, while more difficult and longer trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31464195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cruces Viewpoint – Viewpoint of the Hanging Houses loop from Sorbas&lt;/a&gt;, may take over 4 hours.

Q: What are the elevation changes like on the mountain bike trails?
A: You can expect significant elevation changes on many trails. For instance, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uleila del Campo – Alto de Velefique Pass loop from Uleila del Campo&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1600 meters of ascent, while moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31552508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uleila del Campo loop from Uleila del Campo&lt;/a&gt; feature around 490 meters of climbing.
tips:
Uleila del Campo is a small town located on the southern slopes of the Sierra de los Filabres, at the foot of Monteagud Hill. Its surroundings combine hills, gullies, and unusual sites such as "El Salar," a salt flat with a unique ecosystem. Its heritage includes the Neo-Romanesque Church of Santa María and the Cueva de la Mora archaeological site, home to Neolithic remains and cave paintings.
The town keeps its traditions alive with festivals such as the Santo Cristo de las Penas festival in September, ideal for discovering local life and culture after a hike through the mountains.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing the Transandalus: Uleila del Campo Loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike loop typically takes around 2 hours to complete. However, the actual time can vary depending on your pace, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of dirt tracks, paths, and secondary roads. While generally designed to avoid overly rugged sections, you might encounter sand, streams, and stones, occasionally requiring you to dismount. The section from Uleila del Campo to Senés involves navigating small passes and crossing dry riverbeds.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: For comfortable riding over the varied surfaces and potential potholes, a bike with wide tires and good grip is recommended. A gravel bike or a full-suspension mountain bike would be suitable choices for this loop.

faqs.question: Is the Transandalus: Uleila del Campo Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The loop is categorized as a "moderate mountain bike ride." While shorter excursions might be suitable for any fitness level, completing the entire loop comfortably requires good to very good fitness and advanced riding skills due to varied terrain and some climbing.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a rich tapestry of landscapes, from golden sand and forests to valleys and olive groves. Around Uleila del Campo, you'll see country houses, orchards, and almond trees, with the village of Senés set against the backdrop of the Sierra. The Almería province offers a fascinating variety, including arid landscapes and distant mountains.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this loop?
faqs.answer: Andalusia offers pleasant riding conditions for much of the year. However, spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer. Consider visiting in late winter/early spring to potentially see almond trees in blossom.

faqs.question: Are there any notable features or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop is part of the extensive 2,000-kilometer Transandalus route, celebrated for showcasing the most beautiful areas of Andalusia. You'll pass through the charming settlement of Uleila del Campo itself, and the route offers stunning vistas of the surrounding natural beauty, including views of country houses and almond groves.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Uleila del Campo. You can typically find parking within the village to begin your ride. Specific trailhead parking information may vary, but starting from the village center is a common approach.

faqs.question: Is the Transandalus: Uleila del Campo Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact loop are not detailed, generally, dogs are allowed on many trails in the region. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near livestock, and to carry water for them, particularly in warmer months.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not typically need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to ride the Transandalus: Uleila del Campo Loop. It is part of a public route designed for cycling enthusiasts.

faqs.question: Does this loop intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Transandalus, Camino de Albánchez, Rambla de Góchar, and Camino a Nuestra Señora de Monteagud.
